headline: AI gets more task-focused: OpenAI pushes work agents, Anthropic sharpens safety, and teams get better Slack support
dek: Four recent AI updates point in the same direction: models are being positioned less as chatbots and more as tools that can do longer tasks, plug into real workflows, and support stronger controls.
hook: Three AI updates that actually matter today

stories.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6
stories.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 across ChatGPT, Codex, and its API, with three tiers aimed at different tradeoffs between capability and cost. The company frames the model around stronger reasoning, better efficiency, and support for multi-agent work. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6/))
stories.why_it_matters: For creators and small teams, lower token use and more reliable task completion can matter more than flashy demos, because it affects both cost and how often the model is useful in a real workflow. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6/))
stories.practical_angle: This is relevant if you use AI for drafting, analysis, coding, or automation and want fewer retries and more predictable output. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6/))

stories.title: ChatGPT Work turns ChatGPT into a task runner
stories.summary: OpenAI introduced ChatGPT Work, which can connect to apps and files, create deliverables, and continue multi-step work over time. The desktop app now combines Chat, Work, and Codex, and OpenAI says the experience can use connected tools, browser context, and scheduled tasks. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/))
stories.why_it_matters: This moves AI closer to the place where small businesses actually feel value: repetitive admin, project updates, research, and first-draft asset creation. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/))
stories.practical_angle: If you already use Slack, Gmail, Drive, Teams, or spreadsheets, this points to a future where AI can draft the first pass instead of merely suggesting what to do next. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/))

stories.title: Claude Tag brings AI into Slack
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag in beta for Slack users on Claude Enterprise and Team plans, aiming to make Claude easier to use inside team conversations. ([anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/introducing-claude-tag))
stories.why_it_matters: For many teams, the win is not a bigger model but less friction: if AI can be summoned inside a chat thread, people are more likely to use it for quick drafts, summaries, and decisions. ([anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/introducing-claude-tag))
stories.practical_angle: This is especially useful for small teams that already run work through Slack and want lightweight AI without changing their stack. ([anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/introducing-claude-tag))

narration_text: Three AI updates worth caring about: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6, OpenAI introduced ChatGPT Work, and Anthropic put Claude directly into Slack. The real story is workflow, not hype. If AI can help with reports, briefs, and repetitive admin, small teams get the most value. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6/))
practical_takeaway: Pick one repetitive workflow this week — for example, meeting notes to draft email, customer research to brief, or weekly reporting — and test whether an AI tool can complete the first pass faster than your current process. Keep the task narrow and require human approval before anything is sent or published. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/))
what_to_test_next: Run a 20-minute pilot: connect one data source, ask the AI to produce a single reusable deliverable, and compare time saved plus edit count against your normal workflow. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/))
